title: Dominium Formalization Chain Registry
repo_status: post-Ω/Ξ/Π and post-P-0..P-4 and post-0-0 and post-Λ-0 and post-Λ-1 and post-Λ-2 and post-Λ-3
execution_context_note: Current execution context is post-Λ-3 with Λ-4 as the active prompt. Some earlier continuity docs still retain historical next-step references to Λ-0; this registry follows the explicit current checkpoint and does not treat those stale next-step pointers as semantic authority.

core_definition.summary: Formalization is the lawful process by which knowledge, design, practice, or structure becomes explicit, portable, reviewable, reusable, and lineage-bearing beyond its immediate local context.
why_needed: support reusable machines, parts, and blueprints
support explicit process pipelines and inspection methods
support revision, supersession, and provenance without silent drift
support believable industrial, legal, educational, and civilizational propagation

chain_stages.summary: New or newly useful phenomenon, design, practice, or structure becomes present to an actor or system.
Discovery or emergent pattern is identified as meaningful and potentially reusable.
Useful knowledge or practice is made durable enough for review, transfer, and refinement.
Captured material becomes a stable declared form with defined scope, structure, and intended reuse.
A specified artifact or method is checked against relevant criteria for trust and reuse.
A validated or otherwise sufficiently trusted form becomes an explicit reusable coordination device.
A standard or specification becomes embedded in organizations, traditions, legal structures, or social systems.
A formalized artifact or process spreads into repeated use, manufacture, deployment, or civilizational propagation.
Formalized artifacts and methods are maintained, corrected, extended, forked, or reconciled with explicit lineage.
Later status is assigned explicitly to older or replaced formalized artifacts and methods.

precondition_categories.summary: Sufficient knowledge, records, observations, or measurements exist to advance the stage lawfully.
Required authority, certification, or institutional legitimacy exists for the stage being attempted.
The necessary material, tooling, machine, or infrastructural support exists.
The artifact or method has been explicitly captured with enough clarity for review and reuse.
The relevant validation methods, inspection methods, or test facilities exist.
Supporting schools, factories, bureaucracies, legal systems, or equivalent organizations exist where required.
Where reuse depends on compatibility, the relevant interfaces or protocol commitments exist.

recognition: recognition does not create portable standards by itself
semantic_ascent: formalization stabilizes and propagates emergent meaning rather than replacing ascent
capability_surfaces: formalization may affect which capability surfaces exist or become reusable
capability surfaces still remain distinct from the formalization chain
substitution_and_capsules: formalization may define when substitution is allowed later
formalization alone does not authorize substitution
later_upsilon: later provenance, naming, compatibility, and release/versioning policy must preserve the semantic lineage floor defined here
later version identity policy may extend but may not erase formalization provenance

lambda_5: desires may target discovery, trusted methods, standards, adoption, and long-term maintenance rather than only objects
acceptance and refusal can be mapped against stage progression and precondition gaps
sigma: tasks over blueprints, standards, protocols, and procedures must respect explicit stage boundaries and non-automatic promotion
governance may enforce lineage, validation, and adoption posture without redefining formalization itself
phi: runtime planning infrastructure may assume explicit design, validation, and propagation stages exist
runtime systems may not treat recipe-like shortcuts as a substitute for formalization lineage
upsilon: artifact naming, versioning, compatibility, provenance, and supersession policy must preserve explicit historical identity
release lineage may refine operational metadata without overriding semantic chain stages
